title: Red Hat Security Advisory: cups security update
overview:
An update for cups is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 8.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Moderate. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

full_text:
Red Hat Security Advisory: cups security update

An update for cups is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 8.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Moderate. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

An update for cups is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 8.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Moderate. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

CVE-2025-58436: A flaw was found in cups. A client that connects to cupsd but sends slow messages, e.g. only one  byte per second, delays cupsd as a whole, such that it becomes unusable 
by other clients.

CVE-2025-61915: A flaw was found in cups. A user in group defined by SystemGroup directive in /etc/cups/cups-files.conf can use the cups web ui to change the config 
and insert a malicious line. Then the cupsd process which runs as root will parse the new config and  cause an out-of-bound write.
